articles.title: Inside Govinda and Sunita Ahuja's ₹16 crore Mumbai home: Italian sofas, vastu corners and a tulsi balcony
articles.summary: Sunita Ahuja opened the doors of Govinda's ₹16 crore Mumbai home — a Vastu-designed residence with Italian sofas, Versace candles, world travel souvenirs and a tulsi balcony prayer corner.

articles.title: CBDT notifies Cost Inflation Index of 384 for FY27: Who can claim indexation benefits and how it affects capital gains tax
articles.summary: The CBDT has notified the Cost Inflation Index (CII) at 384 for FY2026-27, a key number used to calculate inflation-adjusted long-term capital gains for eligible taxpayers.

articles.title: 100% US tariff threat over Russian oil: India may have reasons to stay calm
articles.summary: This is not the first attempt by the US to penalise countries purchasing Russian oil. Last year, Washington imposed an additional 25% tariff on India over its imports of Russian crude, before reversing the measure in February.

articles.title: India's first hydrogen train set for launch: All you need to know
articles.summary: India will soon launch its first hydrogen fuel cell train on Friday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will flag off the train in Haryana. This train generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen fuel.

articles.title: Here's what's getting cheaper for consumers, thanks to the India-UK trade deal
articles.summary: UK imported goods worth $949 billion from across the world in FY26; India’s imports to the UK were just $25.12 billion

articles.title: Travel time between Ghaziabad to Noida Airport may go down to 40 minutes. Details here
articles.summary: The Namo Bharat corridor is being designed for a maximum operational speed of 180 kmph, allowing passengers to travel between Ghaziabad and Noida International Airport

articles.title: Can tiny homes solve homelessness? This Canadian entrepreneur's bold idea is making waves
articles.summary: LeBrun's project aims to provide more than just housing. It is designed to help residents rebuild their lives through stable accommodation, employment opportunities and a supportive community

articles.title: Skylines that redefine urban beauty worldwide
articles.summary: Explore the world’s most iconic skylines—from Dubai’s Burj Khalifa to Toronto’s CN Tower—where architecture, culture, and urban design blend into breathtaking city vistas.

articles.title: The secret: What mechanics never tell car owners
articles.summary: Discover the secrets your car mechanic won’t tell you—save money, avoid unnecessary repairs, and learn how to spot hidden parts, DIY fixes, and common service scams.

articles.title: The secret iPhone and Android features that will blow your mind
articles.summary: Unlock hidden iPhone and Android features that boost productivity, simplify navigation, and transform daily device use. From Back Tap to Picture-in-Picture, discover your phone’s secret powers.

articles.title: Top 10 nations with unmatched healthcare excellence
articles.summary: Discover the world’s top 10 nations with unmatched healthcare excellence. From Singapore’s seamless hospitals to Sweden’s universal care, these countries set global standards for health systems.

articles.title: US Fed enlists ex-RBI guv Rajan, 2 other Indians for massive policy overhaul
articles.summary: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan will help shape US monetary policy. He joins a Federal Reserve task force reviewing its policy framework. Rajan will examine balance sheet policy alongside other experts. Other panels will assess data, productivity, and inflation frameworks.

articles.title: At just 2, Bengaluru's Akira has 4 world records—her journey started with simple curiosity
articles.summary: From scooter glides to record books, Bengaluru’s little Akira is proving that age is no barrier to extraordinary talent.

articles.title: SBI appoints former LIC executive as its Chief Financial Officer
articles.summary: The lender said in an exchange filing that Agrawal joined the bank on July 15, and would be Key Managerial Personnel (KMP) of the bank under SEBI regulations.

articles.title: Bank holiday today: Will banks remain shut for Rath Yatra and Harela on July 16? Check state-wise list
articles.summary: Bank branches will be closed in Odisha, Uttarakhand, and Manipur on July 16. These closures are for Ratha Yatra and the Harela festival celebrations. Customers can still use online and mobile banking services for transactions.

articles.title: Stock market Live Updates: BSE Sensex jumps over 150 points, Nifty50 climbs above 24,100 in early trade
Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for July 16, 2026 - check list
articles.summary: Top stock market recommendations: Anant Raj, Sobha Ltd, and PNB Housing Finance have been identified as the top stocks to buy by Aakash K Hindocha, Vice President - Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group/Nuvama Wealth Management for July 16, 2026.

articles.title: 'Very cute': US President Donald Trump welcomes new $1 coin with his face on it
articles.summary: A new US dollar coin will feature President Donald Trump for America's 250th birthday. This commemorative coin is scheduled for release in the autumn of 2026. The design includes Trump's image and traditional American symbols on both sides.

articles.title: China's economy stumbles with growth below target: Report
articles.summary: China's economy slowed more than expected last quarter, reaching its weakest point in over three years. Gross domestic product grew 4.3% from a year ago, missing official targets. This slowdown will likely dominate upcoming Politburo meetings this month.

articles.title: Planning to move to New York? Here's the advice an Indian who has lived there for 10 years wants you to know
articles.summary: Deck: A conversation with content creator Kunal Dutt offers practical lessons on confidence, networking and staying true to your passion while navigating one of the world's toughest cities.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Mukesh Ambani: ‘My big advantage was...’
articles.summary: Under his leadership, RIL has grown into India’s largest private-sector conglomerate, with interests spanning energy, petrochemicals, retail and telecommunications.

articles.title: Union Cabinet clears Rs 1.9 lakh crore push for chips, mobile phones
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved two schemes totaling Rs 1.9 lakh crore for electronics manufacturing. These initiatives aim to boost semiconductor fabrication and mobile phone production significantly.

articles.title: Oil prices extend gain for fourth day, stay beyond $85 amid Middle East chaos
articles.summary: Crude oil prices climbed for a fourth day, exceeding eighty-five dollars per barrel. Investors reacted to escalating United States and Iran conflict and shipping route security. United States strikes on Iran's missile sites and coastal defense positions occurred.

articles.title: TVS arm Home Credit to buy education loan company for Rs 967 crore
articles.summary: Home Credit India will acquire Varthana Finance in an all-cash deal valued at Rs 967 crore. This transaction will make Varthana a wholly owned subsidiary of Home Credit India. The acquisition aims to diversify portfolios and improve business mix for the company.

articles.title: Tata Motors pips M&M to regain No 2 ranking
articles.summary: India's passenger vehicle market recorded its strongest first quarter ever. Domestic sales increased significantly, driven by SUV demand and new model launches. Tata Motors reclaimed the second position, surpassing Mahindra & Mahindra in sales figures.

articles.title: Rising cost fuels rush for refurbished devices
articles.summary: India's refurbished device market is set to significantly outperform new ones this year. Soaring memory costs are pushing up prices and driving consumers toward certified pre-owned alternatives.

articles.title: Ficci chief Anant Goenka: It's for industry to take advantage of FTAs
articles.summary: Economic momentum sustains as oil prices moderate and trade deals offer new opportunities. Corporate sector performance shows robust demand, with automotive and banking sectors leading growth. Manufacturing faces margin stress due to inventory, while IT remains under pressure.

articles.title: India-UK trade deal: British cars get cheaper but Scotch has to wait
articles.summary: British car prices are decreasing as the India-UK CETA agreement begins implementation. Imported Scotch and Indian whiskies will see price reductions after state excise clearances.

articles.title: June jobless rate remains at 5.5%, but women find it hard to get work
articles.summary: The national unemployment rate remained steady at five point five percent in June. Women experienced a rise in joblessness, reaching five point nine percent. Meanwhile, the jobless rate for men saw a slight decrease to five point three percent.

articles.title: ILO: 100 crore covered under social security
articles.summary: India's social protection system now reaches 100 crore people, covering over two-thirds of its population. This significant expansion reflects the government's commitment to inclusive development and social welfare initiatives.

articles.title: New technology helps EPFO clear claims faster
articles.summary: EPFO's new technology is accelerating claim settlements and interest payments for members. Auto-processing now handles eighty-three percent of subscriber claims, a notable improvement.

articles.title: India exports $140 million worth of goods to UK at zero duty on Day 1 of trade pact implementation
articles.summary: India exported over $140 million in goods duty-free to the UK on Wednesday. More than fifty export consignments were flagged off from over twenty locations. These shipments included electronics, pharmaceuticals, and textiles, reaching various global gateways.

articles.title: UK backs India’s security concerns, says trade deal based on trust
articles.summary: The India-UK trade deal commenced, reflecting significant trust and cooperation between nations. This agreement is expected to boost both economies and enhance bilateral trade relations. Discussions also addressed national security and the extradition of financial fugitives.

articles.title: NPCIL denies nuclear safety breach after reports of Kudankulam project drawings leak
articles.summary: The clarification comes amid media reports alleging a breach involving engineering drawings associated with the Kudankulam project, one of India's largest nuclear power stations located in Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: US Senate bill proposes 100% tariffs on India over Russian oil: What it could mean
articles.summary: The bill, brokered by the late Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, targets India, China, Slovakia, Hungary and Azerbaijan—the five largest buyers of Russian oil.

articles.title: RBI wants banks to appoint data owners, data stewards, data custodians
articles.summary: The RBI has unveiled an ambitious blueprint to strengthen data governance across banks and financial institutions, signalling that poor data management is now a key supervisory concern.

articles.title: 'Not aware of anything promised or offered': Gautam Adani denies any deal behind US move to drop criminal case
articles.summary: The affidavit was filed in response to a July 8 order by the US District Court for the Eastern District of New York, which directed Adani to state under oath whether he was aware of any promise, offer, or agreement related to the dismissal of the indictment

articles.title: India-UK CETA kicks in: Kolkata ships first jewellery exports to Britain with zero import duty
articles.summary: The flag-off was part of a simultaneous nationwide launch held in Delhi, Mumbai, Surat, Jaipur, and Chennai

articles.title: From ₹15,000 to ₹36,000: She never became a teacher—but what happened next transformed her life
articles.summary: "From a ₹15,000 school-library salary to ₹36,000 a month through household gig apps, Kiran says technology gave her the choice and the chance to rewrite her future."

articles.title: China's GDP growth slows to its lowest since 2022. Q2 growth slips to 4.3%
articles.summary: The slowdown reflects the continued drag from China's prolonged property market downturn, weak consumer confidence, and rising external risks
